item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like these types of articles with debates about strange situations. It really is interesting. This must be hard to make because of thinking.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been said before, but this could be a great fit into the Antimemetics Division.

item.title: Re: Slight nitpicking
item.content:encoded: Again great job writing this, but to reiterate what Stockform said and to copy/paste from the object class page "Even the mere existence of Thaumiel-class objects is classified at the highest levels of the Foundation and their locations, functions, and current status are known to few Foundation personnel outside of the O5 Council."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a basic concept (and I'm surprised it hasn't been done before), but the execution and emotion keep me from downvote. I was on the fence about no vote or upvote then the last 2 logs pushed me over the edge, it just seems like something that would actually happen. Minor nitpick, would they really allow 5996 to research herself? She might be able to manipulate her containment procedures to allow more entertainment money, or more freedom, etc. Nevertheless, still an upvote. Congrats on your first SCP, great job

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this. I think it is an interesting concept that was well executed. I liked what I saw of 5996's personality and the ethical dilemma it presented, as well as how the other SCP staff dealt with this scenario. The changelog at the end was a very nice touch. However, I think the incident log isn't quite clinical enough for an SCP article and needs some editing.

Just as an example: "Mallowar falls silent and adopts a look of sudden confusion as SCP-5996 unknowingly enters within range of him." is a little clunky. Perhaps it could be changed to something like "SCP-5996 enters (insert range). Mallowar stops speaking, appearing confused."

All in all, I really liked this. Upvoted.

The dialogue in the interview feels a bit off, though I can't exactly put my finger on it - I would suggest that you get some people to read it and give feedback. Also, while it is explained how she does it in the interview, I think that 5596 discovered her anomalous nature too easily. However, those are both fairly minor nitpicks; this entry is otherwise very interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the idea behind this one, it was executed pretty well and the logs were especially interesting to read.

Great work, this is impressive for a first SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems like something that might be good subject matter for an Antimemetics Division tale.

item.title: Slight nitpicking
item.content:encoded:
+1,great job. However, I am an asshole, so I will say that I noticed the following in your article:

"Approximately two hours after this interview concluded, SCP-5996 was given its official designation. Approximately four hours after that, it was moved into its current containment chamber."

To me, this phrase seems a little clunky, and I'm not sure to recommend you remove it, or recommend you rewrite it. It just doesn't sit well with me.

My other minor nitpick is that in one of your logs a normal foundation employee casually references the existence of Thaumiels. Very few people are even supposed to know the object class exists, much less actually be able to reference it to lower level staff. If that was in an interview like this, the entire interview probably would be redacted, since most foundation employees are only supposed to know of three object classes.

Again, great work though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks!

Yeah, I've been running proofread after proofread, trying to find any and all errors, but some always seem to slip through the cracks. Anyone let me know if you find one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really cool concept, and I think it's executed pretty well. I do think it could use another proofread - there were a few spelling and grammatical errors that must've slipped through, and I think the dialogue throughout the logs doesn't flow especially well, but the anomaly is good and I really like SCPs that explore the relationship between the anomaly itself and the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy the concept and the idea behind it, but i feel like the first two logs are too weak for me to upvote. I'll novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty impressed by this, seeing as it's your first SCP

Didn't like the actual SCP that much but the logs save it from a novote,

+1

item.title: Finally Posted!
item.content:encoded:
This has been in the process of greenlighting, drafting, and reviewing for about a month now, so I'm happy to see it done!

It's my first SCP, and while I'm ultimately proud of what I've made, I'm also ready for it to turn out to be garbage, so let me all know your honest opinions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(you may know me as Cephalopod on discord)
Glad to see this has gotten onto the site! I was a fan of the moral dilemma of a researcher becoming an SCP, and I hope it stays afloat from now on!
